steps.title: Identify the locational cue
steps.content: The previous sentence ends: readings “are taken and sent to the International Bureau of Weights and Measures (BIPM) in France.” The blank opens the next sentence, which says BIPM metrologists assemble those readings. The blank must connect the location just named (France / BIPM) to the action that follows there.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each option against the context:
  • Choice A — There,: a locational adverb meaning “at that place” — points directly back to BIPM in France, placing the next action at the same location just mentioned. ✓
  • Choice B — In particular,: signals that what follows is a specific example or detail of the previous point. The sentence is not narrowing down an example; it is describing the next step at a new location. Violates the exemplification rule: “In particular” requires the second idea to be a subset of the first, not a sequel. ✗
  • Choice C — For example,: also signals exemplification. The assembling of readings at BIPM is not an example of sending readings to France; it is what happens afterward. Same exemplification rule violated. ✗
  • Choice D — Conversely,: signals contrast or opposition. There is no contradiction between sending readings to France and the metrologists assembling them there — both sentences describe the same cooperative process. Violates the contrast rule. ✗
A is correct.
